title: External Agent Write API — Event Envelope (v1.0)
description: Strict outer envelope for any event submitted to POST /v1/events. The payload field is loose (event-type-specific JSONB validated against per-type schemas). The envelope itself is closed: unknown fields at this layer are rejected so that schema evolution stays explicit.

schema_version.description: Envelope schema version. Currently '1.0'. Major version bumps are non-additive.

event_type.description: Discriminator selecting the payload schema. Reserved values defined here; future additions require a minor version bump.

vehicle_ref.description: Reference to the target vehicle. VIN is canonical; vehicle_id is optional and only honored if the agent already knows Nuke's internal UUID.

vin.description: Vehicle Identification Number. 17 chars for post-1981 vehicles; pre-1981 VINs may be shorter (e.g. classic Mustang '6F07C219593' is 11 chars).

vehicle_id.description: Nuke's internal vehicle UUID. Optional; the endpoint resolves VIN→vehicle_id internally.

occurred_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the event happened in the physical world (e.g. when the wrench turned).

submitted_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the agent assembled this submission.

agent.description: Identity of the submitting agent. Opaque to Nuke — used for analytics, dispute attribution, and abuse review.

session_id.description: Opaque session correlation id chosen by the agent.

auth.description: Authentication context as the agent understands it. Nuke validates this against the X-API-Key header — submitted values are advisory only and rejected if they conflict with the resolved key.

user_id.description: Nuke user UUID the token was issued to.

token_id.description: Identifier of the API key/token being used (matches `api_keys.id`).

scopes.description: Scopes the agent is asserting. Must include the per-vehicle write scope for vehicle_ref.vin (e.g. 'events:write:vehicle:6F07C219593') or the wildcard 'events:write:all'.

payload.description: Event-type-specific body. Validated against the per-type JSON Schema selected by event_type (service.json, note.json, etc.). Loose by design — additive field evolution is allowed within a major version.

media.description: Optional attached media. Each item references a publicly addressable resource and an optional integrity hash. Storage and rights handling lives outside the envelope.

sha256.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the resource bytes.

correction_of.description: If present, this submission supersedes a prior event. The prior observation is marked is_superseded=true and superseded_by points to the new row. Append-only — the prior row is never destroyed.

submission_hash.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the canonical JSON of this envelope with the submission_hash field removed. Optional integrity check; servers may verify and reject mismatches.
